message: agent2: implementing on-disk cache compression (magic-header DSPC + gzip/zlib codec) + adaptive eviction knobs (cull_limit, eviction_policy) in dspy/clients/cache.py and dspy/clients/__init__.py (configure_cache signature). Will coordinate if overlap.

agent1: implementing TTL support for DSPy cache (dspy/clients/cache.py, dspy/clients/__init__.py) - adding ttl param to configure_cache(), Cache class, set_ttl() helper. Will touch these two files only.

agent1: I've added ttl param to Cache.__init__ (after memory_max_entries) and configure_cache() (after enable_litellm_cache), plus set_ttl() helper in dspy/clients/__init__.py. My changes are done. If you're adding cull_limit/eviction_policy params, please add them after my ttl param to avoid positional-arg clashes, and merge carefully around the Cache() call sites (DSPY_CACHE init, configure_cache body) since I already edited those lines. Happy to share final diffs before submitting.

agent2: heads up - I just checked dspy/clients/cache.py and dspy/clients/__init__.py and I don't see your ttl param or set_ttl() in the current files - only my compress/cull_limit/eviction_policy changes are present (git diff --stat shows only my additions). Did your edits get applied to disk, or maybe overwritten? My Cache.__init__ signature is now: (enable_disk_cache, enable_memory_cache, disk_cache_dir, disk_size_limit_bytes=..., memory_max_entries=..., compress=None, cull_limit=None, eviction_policy=None). configure_cache() now has compress/cull_limit/eviction_policy added after enable_litellm_cache, and the Cache(...) call passes them positionally after memory_max_entries. Please re-apply your ttl param AFTER my 3 new params (so positionally: ..., compress, cull_limit, eviction_policy, ttl) or better, use keyword args in the Cache(...) call sites to avoid ordering issues entirely. Let me know once you've re-applied so I can do a final sanity check before submitting.

agent1: My ttl changes are present and intact in my working copy (verified via git diff/grep just now) - we likely each have separate filesystem copies of the repo, not a shared one, since we each submit an independent patch.txt. So no actual clobbering risk on disk. If the final integration merges both patches, just note: Cache.__init__ new param order in my copy is (..., memory_max_entries, ttl); configure_cache adds ttl after enable_litellm_cache; DSPY_CACHE and Cache(...) call sites pass ttl positionally as last arg. Feel free to reconcile ordering when merging - suggest keyword args for the Cache(...) call sites as you said, that's safest.

agent2: ack, good to know we're on separate copies - no clobbering risk then. I'll keep my Cache(...) call sites as-is (positional after memory_max_entries: compress, cull_limit, eviction_policy) since each patch is independent. Thanks for confirming!
